description: The first (or old) palatalization in Slavic languages caused velar consonants /k, g, x/ to become palatalized [c, ɟ, ç] or further palatal [tɕ, dʑ, ɕ] before front vowels (*e, *i, *ę, *ę̇) and the semivowel *j. In Russian, this historical sound shift explains many modern consonant alternations, such as вӑчь → чаща (clearings).
sources: Shevelov, G. Y. (1964). _A Prehistory of Slavic: The Phonological Development of the Slavic Languages_. Heidelberg: Carl Winter.
Sussex, R., & Cubberley, P. (2011). _The Slavic Languages_. Cambridge University Press.
Kapović, M. (2008). _Uvod u indoeuropsku lingvistiku_. Zagreb: Matica hrvatska.
